evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Uses correct current scopes instagram_business_basic and instagram_business_content_publish. However, uses graph.facebook.com/v17.0 for business account lookup (older version, current is v21.0) and the authorization endpoint api.instagram.com/oauth/authorize is somewhat outdated vs current www.instagram.com/oauth/authorize for IG Login flows. Token exchange flow generally consistent.
evidence.executability: Code blocks parse. Backend callback is a GET that redirects, but frontend callback POSTs JSON to the same backend endpoint — these are inconsistent and the POST won't work against the GET endpoint. Tokens stored by user_id but lookup uses string keys inconsistently. Tests reference real network calls. Not sandbox-blocked but requires multiple fixes.
evidence.completeness: All six pieces present: install bash, env template, backend code, frontend code, test command, API key acquisition. However, backend GET callback vs frontend POST callback is a conflicting sample. Env vars listed match those referenced. REACT_APP_BACKEND_CALLBACK is referenced but the backend doesn't expose a POST endpoint matching it.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Multiple official developers.facebook.com/docs/instagram-platform URLs cited inline near relevant code sections (auth endpoint, token exchange, refresh). Canonical official domain used throughout.

rubric_notes: Backend GET callback contradicts frontend POST — conflicting flow samples. Uses Graph API v17.0 (older but not in deterministic trap list). Scopes are current.
